cn: 时尚行业这种自上而下的观点早已过时了，也与《时尚经济学》一书中描写的狂热世界迥异，该书是伊丽莎白·克莱因对“快时尚”的三年控诉。
note: 在过去的大约十年间，技术的进步已经使得大众服装品牌，如飒拉（Zara）、海恩斯莫里斯（H&M）、优衣库（Uniqlo）等，能够对时尚做出更快速的反应，对需求做出更精准的预测。

en: These labels encourage style-conscious consumers to see clothes as disposable—meant to last only a wash or two, although they don’t advertise that—and to renew their wardrobe every few weeks. (31 words)
cn: 周转更快意味着库存浪费更少、新品发布更多、利润更高。
note: 这些品牌鼓励追求时尚的消费者把衣服当成是一次性用品——买来就是为只穿一两水，尽管他们没有这么宣传——他们还鼓励消费者每隔几周就更新一次衣柜。

en: The victims of this revolution, of course, are not limited to designers. (12 words)
cn: 克莱因说，这些品牌以极其低廉的价格销售时髦的商品，控制了时尚的循环周期，撼动了整个行业，而这个行业曾长期以来习惯了季节更替的步伐。
note: 这场变革的受害者，当然，不只是设计师。

en: Overdressed is the fashion world’s answer to consumer-activist bestsellers like Michael Pollan’s The Omnivore’s Dilemma. (15 words)
cn: 要在全世界其2300多家门店销售一款5.95美元的针织超短裙，海恩斯莫里斯（H&M）就必须要依靠海外廉价的劳动力，大批采购原材料以至于过度消耗自然资源，还大量使用有害的化学物质。
note: 如果说迈克尔·波伦的《杂食者的两难》是消费者激进分子的畅销书，那么《时尚经济学》则是时尚界的同类书籍。

en: Americans, she finds, buy roughly 20 billion garments a year—about 64 items per person—and no matter how much they give away, this excess leads to waste. (28 words)
cn: 批量生产的服装，像快餐，尽管满足了人们的渴望和需求，却不耐用，还浪费，”克莱因如是说。
note: 她发现，美国人每年要买大约200亿件服装（每人约64件），且无论他们捐赠多少，这种无节制都会造成浪费。

en: But as Cline is the first to note, it took Beaumont decades to perfect her craft; her example can’t be knocked off. (22 words)
cn: 在《时尚经济学》的结尾，克莱因给大家介绍了她的典范，一位布鲁克林区的女士，名叫萨拉·凯特·博蒙特，她从2008年起所有衣服都自己做——相当漂亮。
note: 不过正如克莱因首次提到的，博蒙特花了几十年来完善自己的手艺，她的事例不能轻易复制。

en: She exhibits the idealism common to many advocates of sustainability, be it in food or in energy. (17 words)
cn: 尽管几家快时尚公司已经在努力控制其对劳动力和环境的影响——包括海恩斯莫里斯（H&M），他们推行了环保的“可持续时尚”——但克莱因相信只有消费者才能促成持久的变革。
note: 她表现了很多倡导可持续发展的人都具有的理想主义，无论是在食品还是在能源方面。

en:
1302
An old saying has it that half of all advertising budgets are wasted—the trouble is, no one knows which half. (21 words)
cn: 虚荣是一种常态，人们只有在无力支付非理性购物时，才会开始更加理性地购物。
note: 老话说得好，打广告的钱一半都打了水漂——问题是，没人知道究竟是哪一半。

en: By watching what people search for, click on and say online, companies can aim “behavioural” ads at those most likely to buy. (22 words)
cn: 在互联网时代，至少在理论上，浪费的那部分可以大幅减少。
note: 通过观察人们在网上搜索、点击、和发表的内容，企业可以针对最有可能购买的人投放“行为”广告。

en: Or should they have explicit permission? (6 words)
cn: 在过去几周，一场争论已经说明了这种精细信息对于广告商的价值：广告商是否可以认为用户乐意被追踪且接受行为广告？
note: 还是说广告商是否应该得到用户明确的许可？

en: Microsoft’s Internet Explorer and Apple’s Safari both offer DNT; Google’s Chrome is due to do so this year. (18 words)
cn: 2010年12月，美国联邦贸易委员会（FTC）曾提出，应该在网络浏览器上添加“禁止追踪”（DNT）选项，这样用户就可以告诉广告商他们不想被追踪。
note: 微软的IE浏览器和苹果公司的Safari目前都有“禁止追踪”选项；谷歌公司的酷容浏览器（Chrome）今年也会这么做。

en: On May 31st Microsoft set off the row: It said that Internet Explorer 10, the version due to appear with Windows 8, would have DNT as a default. (28 words)
cn: 二月，联邦贸易委员会（FTC）和数字广告联盟（DAA）一直认为，该行业将会加快响应“禁止追踪”要求。
note: 5月31日，微软就激起了争议：称其计划与视窗操作系统Windows 8 一同发布的IE10浏览器上，会把禁止追踪（DNT）设为默认选项。

en: Human nature being what it is, most people stick with default settings. (12 words)
cn: 广告商惶恐不已。
note: 人性使然，大多数用户都会坚持使用浏览器的默认设置。

en: Bob Liodice, the chief executive of the Association of National Advertisers, says consumers will be worse off if the industry cannot collect information about their preferences. (26 words)
cn: 现在很少有人会开启“禁止追踪”，但如果追踪处于关闭状态，它就会一直处于关闭状态。
note: 美国广告协会会长鲍勃·利奥狄斯称，如果该行业不能收集用户偏好，消费者的处境更糟糕。

en: “They’ll get less meaningful, less targeted ads.” (7 words)
cn: 用户看到的广告不会减少，他说。
note: “他们会看到更没有意义，更没有针对性的广告。”

en: Getting a DNT signal does not oblige anyone to stop tracking, although some companies have promised to do so. (19 words)
cn: 广告商作何反应，尚不得知。
note: 收到“禁止追踪”的信号并不会使任何公司停止追踪，尽管有些公司已经承诺如此。

en: Also unclear is why Microsoft has gone it alone. (9 words)
cn: 由于无法分辨有人是真的反对行为广告，还是他们保留了微软的默认设置，有些公司可能会忽视“禁止追踪”信号，一如既往继续追踪。
note: 同样不清楚的是，微软为什么要这样特立独行。

en: If it is trying to upset Google, which relies almost wholly on advertising, it has chosen an indirect method: there is no guarantee that DNT by default will become the norm. (31 words)
cn: 毕竟，微软也有广告业务，它声称其广告业务也遵守“禁止追踪”的要求，尽管还在研究如何遵守。
note: 如果微软是在试图挫败谷歌的话，后者几乎完全依赖广告，那么它选择了一种迂回的方法：无法保证将“禁止追踪”设置为默认的做法会成为行规。

en: Brendon Lynch, Microsoft’s chief privacy officer, blogged: “We believe consumers should have more control.” (14 words)
cn: “禁止追踪”似乎不是视窗操作系统（Windows）8的最大卖点——尽管微软的其他产品在这方面要比谷歌强。
note: 微软首席隐私官布兰登·林奇在博客中写到：“我们觉得消费者应该有更多的控制权。”

en:
1303
Up until a few decades ago, our visions of the future were largely—though by no means uniformly—glowingly positive. (20 words)
cn: 真那么简单吗？
note: 几十年来，我们对于未来的看法，大体上说，尽管谈不上一致，也算是相当地乐观。

en: Now utopia has grown unfashionable, as we have gained a deeper appreciation of the range of threats facing us, from asteroid strike to epidemic flu and to climate change. (29 words)
cn: 科技或许会解决人类的所有问题，使每个人都觉得有作为，有前途。
note: 如今，空想已不合时宜，因为我们对自己面临的种种威胁有了更深刻的认识，无论是小行星撞击，还是流感，再到气候变化。

en: But such gloominess is misplaced. (5 words)
cn: 你甚至可能会认为，人类可能没什么盼头了。
note: 但这样的担心没有必要。

en: Take a broader look at our species’ place in the universe, and it becomes clear that we have an excellent chance of surviving for tens, if not hundreds, of thousands of years. (32 words)
cn: 化石记录表明，许多物种已经存活了数百万年之久——那么我们为什么就不可以呢？
note: 若以更广阔的视野来看看我们这个物种在宇宙中的地位，很显然，我们还是大有希望再活个几万年，如果说到不了几十万年的话。

en: So what does our deep future hold? (7 words)
cn: 如果查阅国际自然保护联盟濒危物种 《红色名录》上“智人”这一条目，你会读到：“列为无危级别，由于该物种分布非常广泛，适应性强，目前数量持续上升，无重大威胁会导致其整体数量下降”。
note: 那么，我们的遥远未来会是什么样子呢？

en: For example, the Long Now Foundation has as its flagship project a mechanical clock that is designed to still be marking time thousands of years hence. (26 words)
cn: 越来越多的研究人员和组织机构正在认真思考这个问题。
note: 比如，恒今基金会（Long Now Foundation）有一个旗舰项目，是一台机械时钟，设计出来是为了能够在几千年后还能记录时间。

en: The potential evolution of today’s technology, and its social consequences, is dazzlingly complicated, and it’s perhaps best left to science fiction writers and futurologists to explore the many possibilities we can envisage. (32 words)
cn: 或许有人一意孤行，推想久远的未来要比思考眼前的未来更为容易。
note: 当今技术的发展潜力机器社会影响非常复杂，或许最好是让科幻小说家和未来学家去探索我们能想象到的种种可能。

en: But take a longer view and there is a surprising amount that we can say with considerable assurance. (18 words)
cn: 这就是我们发行《弧》这本新刊物的原因，它旨在探索不远的将来。
note: 但如果看得更远点，有相当大部分未来的事情，我们可以有很大的把握说清楚。

en: This long perspective makes the pessimistic view of our prospects seem more likely to be a passing fad. (18 words)
cn: 人们常说，看过去，知未来：目前，我们已经充分认识到塑造地球，及我们人类发展史的长期模式，所以我们能够有根有据地预测到未来我们子孙后代的生存环境。
note: 我们曾有一阵对人类的未来感到悲观，着眼未来，似乎瞬间化解了这样的悲观态度。

en: But we are now knowledgeable enough to reduce many of the risks that threatened the existence of earlier humans, and to improve the lot of those to come. (28 words)
cn: 诚然，未来绝非一片光明。
note: 不过，我们已经掌握了足够的知识，有能力去降低曾经威胁过早期人类生存的许多风险，改善后人的命运。

en: But on the more important matter of the Constitution, the decision was an 8-0 defeat for the Administration’s effort to upset the balance of power between the federal government and the states. (32 words)
cn: 周一，最高法院以5：3的投票结果，裁定亚利桑那州移民法多项条款无效——对奥巴马政府来说，这是场小小的政策胜利。
note: 但在涉及宪法这一更重要的问题上，8：0的决议挫败了奥巴马政府想要打破联邦政府和各州之间权力平衡的努力。

en: The Constitutional principles that Washington alone has the power to “establish a uniform Rule of Naturalization” and that federal laws precede state laws are noncontroversial. (25 words)
cn: 在这场亚利桑那州对联邦政府的案例中，亚利桑那州计划要求州警察和地方警察执行联邦移民法，从而引起争议；该计划中有四项条款受到质疑，其中三项被最高法院多数票裁决无效。
note: 宪法原则是：只有华府才有权“制定统一的归化条例”，联邦法律高于各州法律，这两点毋庸置疑。

en: Justice Anthony Kennedy, joined by Chief Justice John Roberts and the Court’s liberals, ruled that the state flew too close to the federal sun. (24 words)
cn: 但亚利桑那很久以前就企图制定与现有联邦法并行的州政策。
note: 大法官安东尼·肯尼迪，首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨，还有法庭的自由派等人裁定，亚利桑那州不知分寸，挑衅联邦政府。

en: However, the Justices said that Arizona police would be allowed to verify the legal status of people who come in contact with law enforcement. (24 words)
cn: 对于已裁决无效的三项条款，法官多数认为，国会早已有意“占地”，故亚利桑那州侵犯了联邦特权。
note: 但是，大法官们都认为，亚利桑那州警察有权核查可疑人员的法律身份。

en: Two of the three objecting Justices—Samuel Alito and Clarence Thomas—agreed with this Constitutional logic but disagreed about which Arizona rules conflicted with the federal statute. (27 words)
cn: 这是因为国会一直期望联邦政府和州政府在移民政策上能联合执法，并明确鼓励州政府官员能与联邦同事之间能信息共享，通力合作。
note: 投反对票的三位大法官中，有两位，即塞缪尔·阿利托和克拉伦斯·托马斯赞成这一宪法逻辑，但对于亚利桑那州哪些法律与联邦法律相抵触，他们却存在分歧。

en: The 8-0 objection to President Obama turns on what Justice Samuel Alito describes in his objection as “a shocking assertion of federal executive power”. (24 words)
cn: 唯一的主要反对来自安东宁·斯卡利亚大法官，他根据早期的《客籍法和惩治叛乱法》，强烈支持州政府特权。
note: 以8: 0反对奥巴马总统的原因，如塞缪尔·阿利托在其反对意见中所说，在于其“太过于维护联邦行政权，令人震惊”。

en: In effect, the White House claimed that it could invalidate any otherwise legitimate state law that it disagrees with. (19 words)
cn: 白宫辩称，亚利桑那州法律与联邦政府的执法优先权相抵触，即使州法律是在一字一句严格执行联邦法律。
note: 事实上，白宫宣称，它可以否决任何合法但它不认同的州级法律。

en: But if Congress wanted to prevent states from using their own resources to check immigration status, it could. (18 words)
cn: 有些权利确实只属于联邦政府，其中包括公民身份控制权，边境管理权。
note: 但是，如果国会想阻止各州利用自己的资源核查移民身份，它能。

en: The Administration was in essence asserting that because it didn’t want to carry out Congress’s immigration wishes, no state should be allowed to do so either. (26 words)
cn: 但从未如此。
note: 奥巴马政府实质上是想认定，因为自己不想执行国会在移民事务上的意愿，也不允许任何州政府去执行。

en:
1401
In order to “change lives for the better” and reduce “dependency”, George Osborne, Chancellor of the Exchequer, introduced the “upfront work search” scheme. (23 words)
cn: 对于这一骇人听闻的要求，每一个大法官都拒绝了，合情合理。
note: 为了“改善生活”，减少“依赖”，财政大臣乔治·奥斯本提出了“找工作预付金”方案。

en: What could be more reasonable? (5 words)
cn: 失业者只有带简历到就业中心，上网注册求职，开始找工作，才有资格得到救济金；然后，他们还应该要每周汇报一次，而不是两周。
note: 还有什么比这更合理呢？

en: There will now be a seven-day wait for the jobseeker’s allowance. (11 words)
cn: 接下来还有貌似更合理的。
note: 现在，求职者要拿到津贴，得等七天。

en: “We’re doing these things because we know they help people stay off benefits and help those on benefits get into work faster.” (22 words)
cn: “头几天应该用来找工作，而不是指望办理失业登记”，他说。
note: 我们做这些事情，是因为我们知道这样做能帮助人们不必靠救济金生活，并帮助那些依靠救济金生活的人更快地找到工作。”

en: Really? (1 words)
cn: 帮助？
note: 真的？

en: What motivated him, we were to understand, was his zeal for “fundamental fairness”—protecting the taxpayer, controlling spending and ensuring that only the most deserving claimants received their benefits. (29 words)
cn: 乍一听，这是一位心系社会的财相啊，他努力让人民生活更美好，还要“改革”一个特别纵容的社会体制，因为这个体制几乎不要求新失业的人去努力找工作，还助长惰性。
note: 不过我们要明白， 驱使他的是他热衷于“基本公平”——保护纳税人，控制开支，确保只有最值得帮助的申请人得到救济金。

en: It is financially terrifying, psychologically embarrassing and you know that support is minimal and extraordinarily hard to get. (18 words)
cn: 失去工作会让人难过：你不可能是心里哼着曲儿，蹦蹦跳跳地去就业中心，想着从慷慨的政府那里能拿到双倍的收入还挺开心。
note: 失业带来的是经济上的恐慌，心理上的尴尬，而且你知道失业津贴少得可怜，也极难获得。

en: Worse, the crucial income to feed yourself and your family and pay the bills has disappeared. (16 words)
cn: 现在，没有工作单位要你；能给你生活带来奋斗目标和秩序的工作环境，你现在也参与不进去。
note: 更糟糕的是，用来养活自己和家人，用来支付账单的主要收入来源，没有了。

en: But in Osborneland, your first instinct is to fall into dependency—permanent dependency if you can get it—supported by a state only too ready to indulge your falsehood. (29 words)
cn: 如果问任何一个刚下岗的人想要什么；回答永远是：一份工作。
note: 但在奥斯本的世界里，你的第一本能会是陷入依赖——如果可能，永远依赖 —— 政府也非常愿意纵容你的错误想法，乐意让你这样依赖。

en: The principle of British welfare is no longer that you can insure yourself against the risk of unemployment and receive unconditional payments if the disaster happens. (26 words)
cn: 这好像20多年来的日益严格的求职和福利管理制度改革从来都没有发生过似的。
note: 为了抵御失业的风险，你可以给自己投保，如果灾难发生，可以获得无条件的资助；英国福利的指导思想不再如此了。

en: Instead, the claimant receives a time-limited “allowance,” conditional on actively seeking a job; no entitlement and no insurance, at £71.70 a week, one of the least generous in the EU. (30 words)
cn: 甚至“求职者津贴”这一说法就是想要把失业者重新定义为“求职者”；求职者没有享受福利的基本权利；而这福利是他或她定期缴纳社会保险换来的。
note: 相反，领救助的人得到的只是一份有时间限制的“补贴”，前提是要积极找工作；没有权利，没有保险，每周71.7英镑，欧盟国家最低。

en: But there are few places where clients have more grounds for complaint than America. (14 words)
cn: 四海之内，律师，与其他行业的从业人员相比，最招人反感——可能新闻行业例外。
note: 然而，没有哪一个国家，其委托人比美国的更有理由抱怨。

en: The best lawyers made skyscrapers-full of money, tempting ever more students to pile into law schools. (16 words)
cn: 经济危机爆发之前的十年间，美国法律服务支出的增长速度是通胀的两倍。
note: 顶级律师挣得盆满钵满，吸引了越来越多的学生扎堆似地涌入法院。

en: Many of them instead become the kind of nuisance-lawsuit filer that makes the tort system a costly nightmare. (18 words)
cn: 但是大多数法学毕业生从来都进不了大律所。
note: 他们之中的很多人只能成为了妨害案件的诉讼律师，这使得民事侵权法律系统成为噩梦，花费高得离谱。

en: One is the excessive costs of a legal education. (9 words)
cn: 很多原因造成了这种情况。
note: 其一是读法学专业花费太大。

en: This leaves today’s average law-school graduate with $100,000 of debt on top of undergraduate debts. (15 words)
cn: 在美国的大多数州，成为律师只有一条路：先在某个不相关的专业读四年本科，然后在美国律师协会认证的200所法学院中选一所，再用三年时间读一个法学学位，最后还要花巨资准备律师资格考试。
note: 这使得如今的法学院毕业生除了读本科的欠款，每人平均还要背负10万美元的债务。

en: Reforming the system would help both lawyers and their customers. (10 words)
cn: 读法学院欠下的债务意味着他们必须拼命地努力工作。
note: 制度改革对律师及其委托人都有好处。

en: One idea is to allow people to study law as an undergraduate degree. (13 words)
cn: 人们早就提出过一些合理的建议，但是州一级的行业管理机构却过于保守，不愿实施。
note: 有人建议允许学生在本科阶段学习法律。

en: If the bar exam is truly a stern enough test for a would-be lawyer, those who can sit it earlier should be allowed to do so. (26 words)
cn: 还有人建议让学生在法学院学习两年后就可以参加律师资格考试。
note: 如果律考对想成为律师的人而言真的足够严格，那就应该允许有能力的人提前参加考试。

en: The other reason why costs are so high is the restrictive guild-like ownership structure of the business. (17 words)
cn: 那些学生，如果不需要额外培训，就能把他们堆积如山的债务减少三分之一。
note: 法律服务花费如此高昂的另一个原因是该行业类似于行会的所有权结构具有很强的约束性。

en: This keeps fees high and innovation slow. (7 words)
cn: 除了在哥伦比亚特区，非执业律师不能持有律师事务所的股份。
note: 这让费用居高不下，改革进展缓慢。

en: In fact, allowing non-lawyers to own shares in law firms would reduce costs and improve services to customers, by encouraging law firms to use technology and to employ professional managers to focus on improving firms’ efficiency. (36 words)
cn: 律师行业内部也迫切要求改革，但是管理层中反对改革的人却坚持认为，不让外行进入律师事务所是为了让律师没有挣钱的压力，从而一心一意为客户服务。
note: 事实上，让非执业律师也能持有律所股份，促进律所运用科技、聘请职业经理人专注于改进律所工作效率，可以降低成本，提高客户服务质量。

en: America should follow. (3 words)
cn: 毕竟，其他国家，比如澳大利亚和英国，已经开始放宽其法律行业的限制。
note: 美国也应该效仿。

en: And it is far from the only one of its type. (11 words)
cn: 300万美元的基础物理学奖的确是一次有趣的尝试，亚历山大·波利亚科夫在三月领取本年度奖项时如是说道。
note: 而该奖项也远非同类奖项中的唯一。

en: Many, like the Fundamental Physics Prize, are funded from the telephone-number-sized bank accounts of Internet entrepreneurs. (16 words)
cn: 正如《自然》杂志上的一篇新闻专题文章所述，近年来有一系列为研究人员设立的巨额奖项，可以比肩诺贝尔奖。
note: 其中许多奖项，比如基础物理学奖，其资金都来源于互联网企业家数额巨大的银行存款。

en: What’s not to like? (4 words)
cn: 据说，这些出资人都在各自领域功成名就，他们想用自己的财富让人们注意到科学领域的成功者。
note: 还有什么不好吗？

en: You cannot buy class, as the old saying goes, and these upstart entrepreneurs cannot buy their prizes the prestige of the Nobels. (22 words)
cn: 多着呢，在新闻专题文章援引的几个科学家看来。
note: 俗话说，金钱买不来地位，这些暴富的企业家也不能给他们自己设立的奖项买来诺贝尔奖的威望。

en: They could distort the achievement-based system of peer-review-led research. (9 words)
cn: 这些新设的奖项是其幕后人士自我炒作的一种手段而已，科学家说。
note: 这些奖项可能会扭曲这个以同行评审为指导的、基于学术成就的研究体系。

en: They do not fund peer-reviewed research. (6 words)
cn: 还可能会使同行评审研究停滞不前。
note: 它们不会资助同行评审研究。

en: The goals of the prize-givers seem as scattered as the criticism. (11 words)
cn: 只会让天才独行的神话永存。
note: 这些人资助奖项，目的不一，对他们的批评也似乎如此。

en: As Nature has pointed out before, there are some legitimate concerns about how science prizes—both new and old—are distributed. (21 words)
cn: 有些人想引起轰动，有些想让大家关注科学，或者想更好地奖励那些投身于科研的人。
note: 正如《自然》杂志曾指出，如何分配科学奖项，无论新旧，大家有些顾虑，也是合情合理的。

en: But the Nobel Foundation’s limit of three recipients per prize, each of whom must still be living, has long been outgrown by the collaborative nature of modern research—as will be demonstrated by the inevitable row over who is ignored when it comes to acknowledging the discovery of the Higgs boson. (51 words)
cn: 今年设立的“生命科学突破奖”，它对生命科学的学科内涵的认定就不具有代表性。
note: 但诺贝尔基金把每个奖项的获奖人数限制在最多三人，且必须在世；现代研究的本质是需要通力合作，这样的限制早已不合时宜，比如，若要承认希格斯玻色子的发现，就会不可避免地产生争执：忽视谁合适？

en: Time, rather than intention, has given them legitimacy. (8 words)
cn: 诺贝尔奖，当然，本身就是当时以为非常富有的个人设立的，用自己的钱，做什么事，他早就决定了。
note: 时间，而非其设立的初衷，赋予了诺贝尔奖正统地位。

en: First, most researchers would accept such a prize if they were offered one. (13 words)
cn: 尽管有些科学家可能对这些新奖项牢骚满腹，但有两件事情似乎是很明了的。
note: 其一，要是有人给，大多数研究人员还是会接受这么一个奖项。

en: It is fair to criticize and question the mechanism—that is the culture of research, after all—but it is the prize-givers’ money to do with as they please. (29 words)
cn: 其二，金钱和关注都引入到科学领域而非别处，当然是好事。
note: 对新设立奖项的运作方式提出质疑和批评都是合情合理的——毕竟科研的文化就是如此——但这些钱是奖项设立者出的，他们想怎么花就怎么花。

en:
1404
 “The Heart of the Matter,” the just-released report by the American Academy of Arts and Sciences (AAAS), deserves praise for affirming the importance of the humanities and social sciences to the prosperity and security of liberal democracy in America. (39 words)
cn: 对于这些奖项，明智的人会心怀感激，欣然接受。
note: 《问题的核心》，美国人文与科学院最近刚发布的这一报告，值得赞扬，因为该报告认为人文与社会科学对于守护美国自由民主的安全和繁荣发展至关重要。

en: In 2010, leading congressional Democrats and Republicans sent letters to the AAAS asking that it identify actions that could be taken by “federal, state and local governments, universities, foundations, educators, individual benefactors and others” to “maintain national excellence in humanities and social scientific scholarship and education.” (46 words)
cn: 遗憾的是，然而，该报告未能指出摆在人文教育面前的危机的实质，结果可能是弊大于利。
note: 在2010年，美国国会民主和共和两党的要员都纷纷致函美国人文与科学院，要求其制定出可以让“联邦、州和地方政府，大学，基金，教育工作者，个人捐赠者等等”采纳的行动措施，以“维护国家在人文和社会科学学术研究和教育领域的卓越地位”。

en: Among the commission’s 51 members are top-tier-university presidents, scholars, lawyers, judges, and business executives, as well as prominent figures from diplomacy, filmmaking, music and journalism. (25 words)
cn: 作为回应，美国人文与社会科学院组建了“人文与社会科学委员会”。
note: 该委员会51位成员中，既有来自外交、电影制作、音乐和新闻等行业的英才，还有顶尖大学的校长、学者、律师、法官和企业家等。

en: Because representative government presupposes an informed citizenry, the report supports full literacy; stresses the study of history and government, particularly American history and American government; and encourages the use of new digital technologies. (33 words)
cn: 报告中指出的目标，总体来说值得称赞。
note: 因为代议制政府预设其民众都是知情公民，所以该报告支持全民教育；强调学习历史和政体，尤其是美国历史、美国政体；鼓励使用新的数字技术。

en: The report also advocates greater study of foreign languages, international affairs and the expansion of study abroad programs. (18 words)
cn: 为了鼓励创新和竞争，报告呼吁加大科研投入；精心设计一套连贯的课程体系，提高学生在 21 世纪具备解决问题和有效沟通的能力；增加对教师的拨款；鼓励学者发挥学识，应对当今巨大的挑战。
note: 该报告还提倡加强外语学习，深入研究国际事务，拓展留学项目。

en: The commission ignores that for several decades America’s colleges and universities have produced graduates who don’t know the content and character of liberal education and are thus deprived of its benefits. (31 words)
cn: 不幸的是，虽历时两年半才拟定完成，《问题的核心》根本没有触及问题的核心：在一流的高校中，人文教育本质上缺乏人文素质。
note: 委员会并没有注意到，几十年来，美国高校培养出的毕业生并不知道人文教育的内涵和特征，因此无法从中受益。

en: Today, professors routinely treat the progressive interpretation of history and progressive public policy as the proper subject of study while portraying conservative or classical liberal ideas—such as free markets or self-reliance—as falling outside the boundaries of routine, and sometimes legitimate, intellectual investigation. (44 words)
cn: 令人遗憾的是，曾经在校园内盛行的探索精神不复存在，取而代之的是把人文和社会科学作为工具，用来宣传“革新”或左翼自由思想。
note: 如今，教授通常把对历史的革新性解读和革新性公共政策视为正统的研究科目，而认为传统或古典的自由主义思想——如自由市场和自力更生，超出常规的，有时甚至是超出了正当的，学术研究范畴。

en: Yet its report may well set back reform by obscuring the depth and breadth of the challenge that Congress asked it to illuminate. (23 words)
cn: 美国人文与科学院对人文教育表现出极大的热情。
note: 然而其报告极有可能延误改革，因为国会令其阐明问题的深度和广度，它并没有弄清楚。

en: But embarrassing scandals and the popularity of the republican left in the recent Euro-elections have forced him to eat his words and stand down. (24 words)
cn: 西班牙国王胡安·卡洛斯曾坚持认为：王，不会退位；至死，方睡。
note: 但尴尬的丑闻，还有共和党左派在最近欧洲选举中大受欢迎，迫使他自食其言，退出王位。

en: Does that mean the writing is on the wall for all European royals, with their magnificent uniforms and majestic lifestyles? (20 words)
cn: 那么，此次西班牙危机是否暗示了君主制已经走向穷途末路？
note: 是否意味着那些穿着华丽的服饰，过着高贵的生活的所有欧洲王室都将厄运临头？

en: When public opinion is particularly polarized, as it was following the end of the Franco regime, monarchs can rise above “mere” politics and “embody” a spirit of national unity. (29 words)
cn: 西班牙的这个情况，让拥护和反对君主制的人都能找到理由。
note: 一旦公众舆论严重对立，弗朗哥政权结束之后就出现过这样的情况，君主就能超越“纯粹的”政治，“体现”国家统一的精神。

en: And so, the Middle East excepted, Europe is the most monarch-infested region in the world, with 10 kingdoms (not counting Vatican city and Andorra). (24 words)
cn: 正是这种明显的超越政治的存在，解释了君主作为国家元首能持续广受欢迎的原因。
note: 也正因此，除中东之外，欧洲处处是君王，举世罕见，多达十个王国，还不算梵蒂冈和安道尔。

en: Even so, kings and queens undoubtedly have a downside. (9 words)
cn: 但与海湾地区和亚洲地区专制的君王不同，欧洲大多数王室之所以都保留了下来，是因为他们能使选民不用劳神费力就可以找到一个毫无争议又令人尊重的公众人物。
note: 即便如此，诸国王，众女王，也总会跌落神坛。

en: At a time when Thomas Piketty and other economists are warning of rising inequality and the increasing power of inherited wealth, it is bizarre that wealthy aristocratic families should still be the symbolic heart of modern democratic states. (38 words)
cn: 尽管他们自称是国家团结的象征，但他们的历史——有时候还包括他们今天的行为——恰好反应了种种陈旧且需要抛弃的特权与不公。
note: 托马斯·皮凯蒂和其他经济学家纷纷都在提醒人们注意，不平等现象在日益加剧，世袭财富带来的权力不断扩大；在这样的背景下，怪异的是，富有的贵族家庭竟然仍是现代民主国家的核心象征。

en: Princes and princesses have day-jobs and ride bicycles, not horses (or helicopters). (12 words)
cn: 最为功成名就的王室家族都在努力摆脱或者隐藏其旧有的贵族气息。
note: 王子公主都有自己的正职，他们骑自行车出行，而不是骑马或者坐直升飞机。

en: While Europe’s monarchies will no doubt be smart enough to survive for some time to come, it is the British royals who have most to fear from the Spanish example. (30 words)
cn: 即便如此，这些富家门第结交的都是全球顶级富翁；并且媒体的打扰，他们要保持自己恰当的形象，也变得越难越难。
note: 欧洲的王室无疑都足够聪明，能在未来一段时间继续存留，但从这次西班牙的例子当中感到最多担忧的，当属英国王室。

en: The danger will come with Charles, who has both an expensive taste of lifestyle and a pretty hierarchical view of the world. (22 words)
cn: 女王，普通老奶奶一样的形象，尽管挺有钱的，只有她，才保全了英国王室的名声。
note: 危险可能在于查尔斯，因为他不仅喜欢奢侈的生活，还认为这个世界等级就是相当分明。

en: Charles ought to know that as English history shows, it is kings, not republicans, who are the monarchy’s worst enemies. (20 words)
cn: 他没能明白到，王室家族之所以能留存下来，很大程度上是因为他们提供了一种服务——作为国家元首，不会引起争议，没有政治倾向。
note: 查尔斯应该知道：国王，而非共和主义者，才是君主制的死敌，英国历史也表明了这一点。

en: The Supreme Court will now consider whether police can search the contents of a mobile phone without a warrant if the phone is on or around a person during an arrest. (31 words)
cn: 宪法到底能在多大程度上保护你的数字资料呢？
note: 目前，最高法院正在考虑，警察在抓捕嫌疑人过程中，没有搜查令，是否可以查看嫌疑人身上或者身旁的手机的内容。/ 警察没有搜查令是否可以查看一部手机的内容，如果在抓捕一个人的过程中，他的手机在手上或者在近旁。

en: It is hard, the state argues, for judges to assess the implications of new and rapidly changing technologies. (18 words)
cn: 加州要求法官废止一项重大裁决，尤其是这项裁决会推翻旧有的共识——警方在逮捕嫌疑人时可以对其财务进行搜查。
note: 加州认为，法官很难评估快速发展的新技术可能带来的影响。

en: Enough of the implications are discernable, even obvious, so that the justices can and should provide updated guidelines to police, lawyers and defendants. (23 words)
cn: 要是最高法院听从了加州的建议，谦虚有余，谨慎不足。
note: 新技术产生的影响之大，足以让人看清，甚至相当明显；因此法官能够而且也应该为警方、律师和被告提供与时俱进的指导原则。

en: The court has ruled that police don’t violate the Fourth Amendment when they go through the wallet or pocketbook of an arrestee without a warrant. (25 words)
cn: 他们应该首先抛弃加州那种蹩脚的观点：智能手机是一个庞大的数据信息库，翻手机类似于，比如说，翻嫌疑人的钱包。
note: 最高法院已经裁定，警方若没有搜查令搜查被捕人的钱包或钱夹并不违反宪法第四修正案。

en: A smartphone may contain an arrestee’s reading history, financial history, medical history and comprehensive records of recent correspondence. (18 words)
cn: 但翻人手机更像是进人家里。
note: 被捕人的智能手机里可能有其阅读记录，财务记录，病历和近期通信往来的详细记录。

en: Americans should take steps to protect their digital privacy. (9 words)
cn: 同时，“云计算”的发展，使得这样搜查手机甚至变得更容易。
note: 美国人应该采取措施保护其数字隐私。

en: Citizens still have a right to expect private documents to remain private and protected by the Constitution’s prohibition on unreasonable searches. (21 words)
cn: 但把敏感信息保存在这些设备上，逐渐成为日常生活的需要。
note: 公民仍有权要求其私人文件应保持隐私状态，并且受宪法保护以避免受到无理的搜查。

en: In many cases, it would not be overly burdensome for authorities to obtain a warrant to search through phone contents. (20 words)
cn: 情况往往都是如此，规定了做事情的原则，也很难划定实际的界限。
note: 很多情况下，警方要获得搜查手机内容的搜查令并不太费力。

en: The court, though, may want to allow room for police to cite situations where they are entitled to more freedom. (20 words)
cn: 一旦碰到严重的紧急状况，他们仍会越过第四修正案的保护条例；他们还会在等待搜查令的时候，采取合理的措施以确保手机数据不会被删除或者篡改。
note: 尽管如此，或许最高法院还想给警方留点余地，好让他们列举出一些可以享有更多行动自由的情况。

en: New, disruptive technology sometimes demands novel applications of the Constitution’s protections. (11 words)
cn: 但对于加州的意见，法官不应该生吞活剥。
note: 全新的，颠覆性的技术有时候需要以新的方式运用宪法保护条例。

en:
1503
 The journal Science is adding an extra round of statistical checks to its peer-review process, editor-in-chief Marcia McNutt announced today. (20 words)
cn: 21世纪数字信息爆炸式增长，很容易就可以获取，法学教授奥林·科尔将此与20世纪把汽车确立为日常生活必需品作比较：当时法官不得不针对私家车这个新的领域制定新的规定；现在他们也不得不解决如何把宪法第四修正案适用于数字信息这一问题。
note: 《科学》杂志主编玛西娅·麦克纳特今天宣布，该杂志拟对同行评审程序额外增加一轮统计数据审查。

en: “Readers must have confidence in the conclusions published in our journal,” writes McNutt in an editorial. (16 words)
cn: 这一规定是在效仿其他杂志的类似举措，之所以这样规定，是因为人们普遍担心数据分析中的基本错误导致许多已经发表的研究成果无法复制。
note: “读者必须对发表在我们杂志上的研究结论有信心，”麦克纳特在一篇社论中写到。

en: Manuscripts will be flagged up for additional scrutiny by the journal’s internal editors, or by its existing Board of Reviewing Editors or by outside peer reviewers. (26 words)
cn: 《科学》杂志与美国统计协会合作，任命了七名专家组成了一个统计数据核查编辑委员会（SBoRE）。
note: 杂志内部编辑、现有评审编委会、或者外部同行评审会对原稿做出标示，以接受额外的彻底审查。

en: Asked whether any particular papers had impelled the change, McNutt said: “The creation of the ‘statistics board’ was motivated by concerns broadly with the application of statistics and data analysis in scientific research and is part of Science’s overall drive to increase reproducibility in the research we publish.” (48 words)
cn: 统计数据核查编辑委员会专家小组会找外面的统计学家来审查这些原稿。
note: 问及是否有什么特定的论文促成了这次变化，麦克纳特说：“成立‘统计数据检查委员会’的动机，开始是因为我们广泛关注统计数据和数据分析在科学研究领域的应用，现在则是因为《科学》杂志致力于全面提高本刊发表论文的可复制性，成立‘统计数据检查委员会’就是其中的一环。”

en: He agreed to join because he “found the foresight behind the establishment of the SBoRE to be novel, unique and likely to have a lasting impact. (26 words)
cn: 哈佛大学公共卫生学院的生物统计学家乔瓦尼·帕尔米加尼是统计数据核查编辑委员会的成员。他说，他期望该委员会“主要起到咨询的作用”。
note: 他之所以同意加入，是因为他“觉得成立这个编辑委员会有先见之明，新颖独特，并可能带来长期的影响”。

en: John Ioannidis, a physician who studies research methodology, says that the policy is “a most welcome step forward” and “long overdue”. (21 words)
cn: 这种影响不仅会遍及《科学》自己的文章，还会波及一大批想效法《科学》杂志的其他出版单位。
note: 约翰·约阿尼迪斯医生专攻研究方法论，他说，该项规定是“一种进步，令人欣慰”，且“早该如此”。

en: I think that, for the majority of scientific papers nowadays, statistical review is more essential than expert review,” he says. (20 words)
cn: “大多数期刊在统计数据审核方面都很薄弱，这有导致其发表论文质量下降。
note: 我认为，对于目前大多数科研论文而言，统计数据审查比专家审查更重要。”他这样说道。

en: Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyze data, but statistical errors are alarmingly common in published research, according to David Vaux, a cell biologist. (26 words)
cn: 不过他也指出，生物医学类期刊，如《内科学年鉴》、《美国医学协会杂志》、和《柳叶刀》等，都格外重视统计数据审核。
note: 专业的科学家应该知道如何分析数据，但根据细胞生物学家大卫·沃克斯的看法，统计错误在已发表的研究中相当普遍，令人担忧。

en: Vaux says that Science’s idea to pass some papers to statisticians “has some merit, but a weakness is that it relies on the board of reviewing editors to identify ‘the papers that need scrutiny’ in the first place”. (38 words)
cn: 研究人员应该提高自己的研究水平，他在2012年写道，但是杂志社的立场也应该更坚定，“聘请通晓统计学的评审和能够核查整个评审过程的编辑”。
note: 沃克斯说，《科学》想把部分论文交给统计学家审核，“有可取之处，但不足之处在于它首先得靠评审编辑委员会来确定‘哪些论文需要仔细审查’”。

en: Integrity had collapsed, she argued, because of a collective acceptance that the only “sorting mechanism” in society should be profit and the market. (23 words)
cn: 两年前，鲁伯特·默多克之女伊丽莎白曾说到过，“众多机构都缺乏诚信，令人担忧”。
note: 诚信的价值观依然瓦解，她争辩说，因为大家都认为，社会唯一的“分选机制”应该是利润和市场。

en: Driving her point home, she continued: “It’s increasingly apparent that the absence of purpose, of a moral language within government, media or business could become one of the most dangerous goals for capitalism and freedom.” (35 words)
cn: 但“正是我们，人类，我们人民大众创造了我们想要的社会，而不是利润”。
note: 为阐明自己的观点，她接着说道：“政府、媒体、或企业等内部价值的缺失，道德语言的缺失，或将成为资本主义和自由最危险的目标之一；这一点日益明显。”

en: As the hacking trial concludes—finding guilty one ex-editor of the News of the World, Andy Coulson, for conspiring to hack phones, and finding his predecessor, Rebekah Brooks, innocent of the same charge—the wider issue of dearth of integrity still stands. (42 words)
cn: 她认为，同样的道德价值缺失问题正在危害新闻国际这样的公司，使其更有可能迷失方向，正如它此前在大规模电话窃听事件中一样。
note: 电话窃听案件审判结果出炉：裁定《世界新闻报》前编辑 安迪·库尔森因合谋窃听电话有罪，其前任丽贝卡·布鲁克斯同诉无罪；如该案结论表明，更大的问题依然存在：诚信缺失。

en: This is hacking on an industrial scale, as was acknowledged by Glenn Mulcaire, the man hired by the News of the World in 2001 to be the point person for phone hacking. (32 words)
cn: 据悉，记者曾窃听多达5500人的电话。
note: 这是一场工业规模的窃听行为，格伦·穆凯尔承认，他 2001 年受雇于《世界新闻报》，是电话窃听的关键人物。

en: This long story still unfolds. (5 words)
cn: 其他人还在候审。
note: 这一连串事件还在发酵。

en: One of the astonishing revelations was how little Rebekah Brooks knew of what went on in her newsroom, how little she thought to ask and the fact that she never inquired how the stories arrived. (35 words)
cn: 我们可以从很多方面看出，道德价值缺失，不仅体现在如此大规模的电话窃听的事实上，还体现在审判这一案件所使用的条款上。
note: 其中揭露出的惊人真相是，丽贝卡·布鲁克斯对发生在其新闻编辑室的情况几乎一无所知，她也几乎没有想到要去过问，而且事实上是她从来没有询问过这些报道的来源。

en: In today’s world, it has become normal that well-paid executives should not be accountable for what happens in the organizations that they run. (23 words)
cn: 她辩护成功的关键，就是她一无所知。
note: 在当今社会，待遇优厚的高管对自己经营的公司所发生的事情不负责任，成了常态。

en: For a generation, the collective doctrine has been that the sorting mechanism of society should be profit. (17 words)
cn: 或许我们不该如此大惊小怪。
note: 一代人了，他们集体的信条就是，社会的分选机制就应该是利益。

en: Words degraded to the margin have been justice, fairness, tolerance, proportionality and accountability. (13 words)
cn: 有点分量的字眼是：效率、弹性、股东价值、商业友好、创造财富、销售额、影响力，还有报纸的发行量。
note: 而正义、公平、宽容、均衡和责任成了边缘词汇。

en: It was to ruin lives in the quest for circulation and impact. (12 words)
cn: 编辑《世界新闻报》的目的，不是为了加强读者的理解认知能力，不是为了在报道中追求正义，也根本不是为了彰显共同的人性。
note: 而是为了追求发行量和影响力，要破坏人们的生活。

en:
1601
France, which prides itself as the global innovator of fashion, has decided its fashion industry has lost an absolute right to define physical beauty for women. (26 words)
cn: 布鲁克斯女士可能曾经，也或许未曾怀疑过她的记者是怎样获得新闻题材的，但是她从不过问，从不指示——当然也从来没有得到过有线索有记录的回答。
note: 法国，是全球时尚创新者，并引以为傲，如今已经裁定其时尚业不再拥有定义女性形体美的绝对权。

en: The parliament also agreed to ban websites that “incite excessive thinness” by promoting extreme dieting. (15 words)
cn: 其立法机构上周初步批准了一项法规：雇用超瘦 T 台模特可能会犯罪。
note: 议会还同意取缔那些靠提倡极端节食的方式来“鼓动人们暴瘦下来”的网站。

en: They suggest beauty should not be defined by looks that end up with impinging on health. (16 words)
cn: 这些举措鼓舞人心，达到了如下几个目的。
note: 这些举措表明，美，不应该靠外表来定义，因为只求外表，最终会有损于健康。

en: And the ban on ultra-thin models seems to go beyond protecting models from starving themselves to death—as some have done. (21 words)
cn: 这是其一。
note: 另外，针对超瘦模特的禁令似乎不仅仅是保护模特，以免他们节食过度致死——还真有这样的事。

en: The bans, if fully enforced, would suggest to women (and many men) that they should not let others be arbiters of their beauty. (23 words)
cn: 女性，尤其是十几岁的少女，他们必须使用某种社会准确来决定其个人价值，对此，时尚行业能给他们发出相关信号予以指引，禁令要求时尚行业要对其发出的信号承担责任。
note: 这些禁令，如果彻底执行，将向女性（以及很多男性）表明，自己的美不应该由他人的裁决。

en: The French measures, however, rely too much on severe punishment to change a culture that still regards beauty as skin-deep—and bone-showing. (22 words)
cn: 或许没那么明显，但他们向大家暗示的是，应该注重性格、才智等无形的品质，而不是一味节食，追求零码或者蜂腰。
note: 法国的这些措施，然而，太过于依赖于靠重罚来改变那种追求外在美、骨感美的文化。

en: The fashion industry knows it has an inherent problem in focusing on material adornment and idealized body types. (18 words)
cn: 根据该项法律，雇佣未达到政府规定体重指数的时尚模特将会导致85000美元罚款和六个月监禁。
note: 时尚行业也知道，自身固有的问题是，太注重物质装饰和理想体型。

en: In contrast to France’s actions, Denmark’s fashion industry agreed last month on rules and sanctions regarding age, health, and other characteristics of models. (23 words)
cn: 在丹麦、美国、以及其他几个国家，时尚行业正试图就模特和时尚形象制定一些非强制性的标准，标准的实施主要依靠同辈压力来起到作用。
note: 与法国的举措既然不同，上个月，丹麦的时尚行业就模特的年龄、健康及其他特征的规定和处罚达成了一致。

en: The charter’s main tool of enforcement is to deny access for designers and modeling agencies to Copenhagen Fashion Week (CFW), which is run by the Danish Fashion Institute. (28 words)
cn: 新修订的《丹麦时尚道德宪章》明确声明：“我们深知，时尚行业对人们理想身材的观念，尤其是对年轻人影响重大，我们以此为己任。”
note: 该宪章的主要执行手段是，拒绝设计师和模特中介公司参加丹麦时装协会组织的“哥本哈根时装周（CFW）”。

en: Relying on ethical persuasion rather than law to address the misuse of body ideals may be the best step. (19 words)
cn: 但总体来说靠的是点评批评的方法让人遵守。
note: 靠道德劝说而不是法律，来解决理想身材标准滥用问题，可能是最好的措施。

en:
1602
For the first time in history more people live in towns than in the country. (15 words)
cn: 若要好上加好，就得帮助人们提高对美的认识，超越特定行业的有形标准。
note: 史上第一次，城里住的人比乡村多。

en: While polls show Britons rate “the countryside” alongside the royal family, Shakespeare and the National Health Service (NHS) as what makes them proudest of their country, this has limited political support. (31 words)
cn: 在英国，这一现象产生了一种奇怪的结果。
note: 尽管民调显示，英国人认为“乡村”，与皇室、莎士比亚和国民保健制度一样，是自己国家最引以为傲的，但这得到的政治支持却比较有限。

en: It was specifically to provide city dwellers with spaces for leisure where they could experience “a refreshing air.” (18 words)
cn: 一个世纪前，奥克塔维娅·希尔牵头成立了国民信托，她不是想要挽救哪些别具特色的住宅，而是想保护“属于每个人的永久自然美景”。
note: 尤其是要给城市居民提供一个休闲场所，好让他们能够享受“清新的空气”。

en: They don’t make countryside any more, and every year concrete consumes more of it. (14 words)
cn: 希尔后来不断呼吁，终于建立了国家公园和绿化带。
note: 他们不再建设乡村，每年混凝土都在不断蚕食乡村。

en: At the next election none of the big parties seem likely to endorse this sentiment. (15 words)
cn: 乡村，需要持久的保护
note: 在下一届选举中，几大党派似乎没有一个赞成这种观点。

en: The concept of sustainable development has been defined as profitable. (10 words)
cn: 保守党的改革计划明确支持农村开发优先于农村保护，甚至在当地居民反对的地方批准建期房。
note: 有人认为，可持续发展的概念就是有利可图。

en: The Liberal Democrats are silent. (5 words)
cn: 同样，工党也想在当地议会反对开发的地方终止地方规划。
note: 自由民主党则保持沉默。

en: Its Campaign to Protect Rural England struck terror into many local Conservative parties. (13 words)
cn: 只有独立党，察觉到了这个机会，与那些主张合理利用绿地的人站在了一起。
note: 其“保护英国乡村运动”使得许多地方保守党胆战心惊。

en: The London agents Stirling Ackroyd recently identified enough sites for half a million houses in the London area alone, with no intrusion on green belt. (25 words)
cn: 要建造新房、工厂和办公楼，合理的地方是有人的地方，比如在基础设施齐全的城镇。
note: 伦敦地产代理公司斯特林-艾可罗伊德仅在伦敦地区就找到了足够开发50万套住宅的地皮，且完全不会侵占绿化带。

en: The idea that “housing crisis” equals “concreted meadows” is pure lobby talk. (12 words)
cn: 伦敦能这样，其他地方更是如此。
note: “住房危机”就等同于“绿地上建房”，这样的观点纯粹是游说团体的说辞。

en: Under lobby pressure, George Osborne favours rural new-build against urban renovation and renewal. (13 words)
cn: 问题不是需要建更多的房子，而是，且一直都是，在哪里建房。
note: 迫于游说团体的压力，乔治·奥斯本更倾向于在乡村新建楼房，而不是在城市改造翻新。

en: This is not a free market but a biased one. (10 words)
cn: 他支持在城外建购物中心，而不是发展商业街。
note: 这不是一个自由市场，而是一个偏见市场。

en: They do so best where building sticks to their edges and respects their character. (14 words)
cn: 乡村小镇和村庄已经发展了起来，并且会一直发展。
note: 有些乡村，坚持优势，尊重特色，发展得最好。

en: Why ruin rural ones? (4 words)
cn: 我们没有破坏城里的保护区。
note: 为什么要去破坏乡村的呢？

en: After the Netherlands, Britain is Europe’s most crowded country. (9 words)
cn: 开发应该是有规划的，而不是任意妄为。
note: 英国是欧洲人口最密集的国家，仅次于荷兰。

en: There is no doubt of the alternative—the corrupted landscapes of southern Portugal, Spain or Ireland. (16 words)
cn: 半个世纪的城乡发展计划使英国能保持令人羡慕的乡村和谐；同时还能实现低密度的城市生活。
note: Avoiding this rather than promoting it should unite the left and right of the political spectrum. (16 words)

en: But even if you accept Friedman’s premise and regard corporate social responsibility (CSR) policies as a waste of shareholders’ money, things may not be absolutely clear-cut. (26 words)
cn: “企业，有，且仅有一种社会责任，”诺贝尔经济学奖获得者米尔顿·弗里德曼写到，“那就是，利用资源，参与各种活动，增加利润”。
note: 但即使你接受弗里德曼的观点，并且还认为企业社会责任（CSR）政策是在浪费股东的钱，事情也可能并非是绝对的泾渭分明。

en: The largest firms in America and Britain together spend more than $15 billion a year on CSR, according to an estimate by EPG, a consulting firm. (26 words)
cn: 新的研究表明，企业社会责任（CSR）可以为公司创造货币价值——至少在其受到腐败指控时是如此。
note: 根据EPG咨询公司的预测，英国两国最大型的公司每年总共在“企业社会责任”上的支出超过1500亿美元。

en: First, consumers may take CSR spending as a “signal” that a company’s products are of high quality. (17 words)
cn: 这能从三个方面增加公司的价值。
note: 其一，消费者或许会认为“企业社会责任”上的花费就可以表明一个公司的产品质量高。

en: And third, through a more diffuse “halo effect,” whereby its good deeds earn it greater consideration from consumers and others. (20 words)
cn: 其次，顾客可能更愿意购买某个公司的产品，认为这是一种间接的方式捐赠给公司扶持的善事。
note: 第三，通过一种能影响到更多人的“光环效应”，其善举赢得了消费者和其他人更多的尊重。

en: A recent study attempts to separate them by looking at bribery prosecutions under America’s Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA). (19 words)
cn: 以前对“企业社会责任”的研究就没有区分这些效应，因为消费者可能同时会受到上述三个方面的影响。
note: 最近的一项研究分析了美国《反海外腐败法》下的行贿诉讼案件，试图区分这三种影响。

en: The study found that, among prosecuted firms, those with the most comprehensive CSR programmes tended to get more lenient penalties. (20 words)
cn: 这项研究认为，由于检察官不会去消费某一公司的产品作为其调查的一部分，他们可能只受到了光环效应的影响。
note: 研究发现，在被起诉的公司里，“企业社会责任”项目全面的企业往往会得到宽大处理。

en: In all, the study concludes that whereas prosecutors should only evaluate a case based on its merits, they do seem to be influenced by a company’s record in CSR. (29 words)
cn: 他们的分析排除了这样的可能性，即：是公司的政治影响力，而非公司在“企业社会责任”上的作为，使他们得到较轻的惩罚；因为那些在整治活动上投入更多的公司并没有获得轻罚。
note: 总之，该项研究得出的结论是，尽管检察官应该只根据案件本身的情况来评判，但他们似乎的确受到了公司“企业社会责任”记录的影响。

en: Researchers admit that their study does not answer the question of how much businesses ought to spend on CSR. (19 words)
cn: 一位研究人员说：“我们估计，消除诸如雇佣童工等重大劳工问题，或是把企业捐赠数额提高20%，都能使企业受到较轻的惩罚，这比贿赂外国官员受到的常规惩罚要低40%。”
note: 研究人员承认，其研究并没有回答企业该在“企业社会责任”上花费多少这一问题。

en: But at least they have demonstrated that when companies get into trouble with the law, evidence of good character can win them a less costly punishment. (26 words)
cn: 该研究也没有表明，企业在制定慈善政策时，在多大程度上指望光环效应，而不是靠其他可能的慈善活动。
note: 但他们至少证明了，一旦公司陷入法律纠纷，品行良好的证据会使其获得较轻的惩罚。

en: Exactly when that day will be is a matter of debate. (11 words)
cn: 总有一天，《纽约时报》会停止刊发纸质媒体报道。
note: 确切是哪一天，还众说纷纭。

en: Nostalgia for ink on paper and the rustle of pages aside, there’s plenty of incentive to ditch print. (18 words)
cn: “会是将来某一天”，该报出版人早在2010年曾说过。
note: 撇开人们对纸上油墨和翻看报纸沙沙作响的怀旧之情不谈，有太多因素促使我们放弃印刷。

en: Readers are migrating away from print anyway. (7 words)
cn: 印刷纸版实体包子所需的基础设施，如印刷机、送货车，不仅仅是贵；而且在当下显得有点多余，因为那些只做在线报纸的竞争对手并不用承受这一系列同样的财务负担。
note: 无论如何，纸质版报纸的读者正在流失。

en: Overhead may be high and circulation lower, but rushing to eliminate its print edition would be a mistake, says BuzzFeed CEO Jonah Peretti. (23 words)
cn: 尽管纸质版的广告销售额远超网络版和手机版，但是纸版的收入还在不断下滑。
note: 运营费用可能很高，发行量可能很低，但急于砍掉纸质版将会是个错误的决定，美国新闻网站百资得（BuzzFeed）的首席运营官乔纳·佩雷蒂这样说道。

en: “Figuring out a way to accelerate that transition would make sense for them,” he said, “but if you discontinue it, you’re going to have your most loyal customers really upset with you.” (32 words)
cn: 佩雷蒂认为，《纽约时报》不应该在剥离纸质版业务上浪费时间，除非他们能恰当处理这件事情。
note: “他们得找到办法加速这一转型，才是明智的，”他说，“但如果停止纸版业务，真的会让最忠实的读者感到失望。”

en: Peretti gives the example of Netflix discontinuing its DVD-mailing service to focus on streaming. (14 words)
cn: 不管怎样，改变，是值得的。
note: 佩雷蒂拿网飞公司来举例说明，网飞公司终止其DVD邮寄服务，转而聚焦流媒体。

en: The move turned out to be foresighted. (7 words)
cn: “当时认为是失误，”他说。
note: 后来才发现此举有先见之明。

en: “ I wouldn’t pick a year to end print,” he said，“I would raise prices and make it into more of a legacy product.” (25 words)
cn: 那要是佩雷蒂执掌《纽约时报》呢？
note: “我不会选择某一年来结束纸质报纸”，他说，“我会提高价格，并将其打造成为更像是一种怀旧产品”。

en: “So if you’re overpaying for print, you could feel like you were helping,” Peretti said. (15 words)
cn: 佩雷蒂的理念是，最忠实的顾客依然会买他们钟爱的产品，而且他们会感觉像是在帮助维护自己信赖的产品的质量。
note: “所以，如果你多付钱买纸质版报纸，你会感觉自己是在帮忙，”佩雷蒂这样说道。

en: In other words, if you’re going to make a print product, make it for the people who are already obsessed with it. (22 words)
cn: “然后逐年涨价，实质上是想要创造额外的收入”。
note: 换言之，如果你想做纸质版报纸，那就最好专门做给那些迷恋纸版的人。

en: Getting the print edition seven days a week costs nearly $500 a year - more than twice as much as a digital-only subscription. (23 words)
cn: 而《纽约时报》或许就是这么做的。
note: 一周七天订阅纸质版报纸，每年要花大概500美元，是纯电子版订阅费的两倍多。

en: “But we’re going to have questions like that where we have things we’re doing that don’t make sense when the market changes and the world changes. (26 words)
cn: “但这绝非易事，并且很庆幸百资得没有这样的传统产业”，佩雷蒂说。
note: “但是，市场在变，世界也在变，我们却还在做一些没有意义的事情，那我们也会遇到类似的事情。

en:
1701
First two hours, now three hours—this is how far in advance authorities are recommending people show up to catch a domestic flight, at least at some major U.S. airports with increasingly massive security lines. (35 words)
cn: 若真如此，积极进取会更好一点。”
note: 开始是两个小时，现在三个小时——这就是官方建议我们赶国内航班须提前抵达的时间，至少在安检队伍越来越长的美国几大主要机场得如此。

en: The crash of EgyptAir Flight 804, which terrorists may have downed over the Mediterranean Sea, provides another tragic reminder of why. (21 words)
cn: 美国人愿意忍受耗费时间的安检程序，以期获得更高的安全水平。
note:
埃及航空804航班坠毁，疑遭恐怖分子袭击沉没于地中海，这一悲剧再次提
醒人们愿意这样做的原因。

en: And it should: Wasted time is a drag on Americans’ economic and private lives, not to mention infuriating. (18 words)
cn: 但是，对航空旅客要求太多，提供相应的安全保障又太少，都会削减公众对安检程序的支持。
note: 会的。时间浪费了，拖累了美国经济，连累了私人生活，更别提有多让人恼火。

en: Enhanced security measures since then, combined with a rise in airline travel due to the improving economy and low oil prices, have resulted in long waits at major airports such as Chicago’s O’Hare International. (34 words)
cn: 去年，美国运输安全管理局（TSA）在一次秘密检查中发现，穿便衣的调查人员能偷偷携带武器，真的假的都能，通过机场安检，几乎屡试不爽。
note: 从那之后加强了安全措施，还有因经济好转、油价降低带来的航空出行增加，导致几大机场大排长龙，比如芝加哥奥黑尔国际机场。

en: Part of the issue is that the government did not anticipate the steep increase in airline travel, so the TSA is now rushing to get new screeners on the line. (30 words)
cn: 航线安全提升了多少，尚不明确，但是排队的长线显而易见。
note: 部分问题在于，政府并没有预料到航空出行会急剧增加，所以运管局现在正忙不迭地招募新的机场安检员。

en: Another factor may be that more people are trying to overpack their carry-on bags to avoid checked-baggage fees, though the airlines strongly dispute this. (24 words)
cn: 部分问题在于，机场就只有那么点空间可以用作安检通道。
note: 另一个因素可能是更多人为了避免缴纳托运行李的费用，会尽量把自己的随身行李装到装不下了，尽管航空公司强烈反对这样做。

en: PreCheck is supposed to be a win-win for travelers and the TSA. (12 words)
cn: 有一项措施运管局可以采取，不需要改造机场，也不需要匆忙招聘安检：把更多的人纳入预检计划。
note: 预检计划，对于乘客和运管局来说，本该是一种双赢。

en: This allows the TSA to focus on travelers who are higher risk, saving time for everyone involved. (17 words)
cn: 通过背景调查的乘客，有资格使用快速安检通道。
note: 这可以使运管局专注于那些高危旅客，节约了所有相关人员的时间。

en: It has not gotten anywhere close to that, and one big reason is sticker shock: Passengers must pay $85 every five years to process their background checks. (27 words)
cn: 运管局计划把2500万人纳入预检。
note: 实际情况差了十万八千里，一个原因是定价太高：乘客每5年必须缴纳85美金来支付其背景调查费用。

en: Upcoming reforms might bring the price to a more reasonable level. (11 words)
cn: 从一开始，这个标价就是预检项目的致命缺点。
note: 或许即将进行的改革会把价格定位在一个更合理的水平。

en: The TSA cannot continue diverting resources into underused PreCheck lanes while most of the traveling public suffers in unnecessary lines. (20 words)
cn: 但是国会应该直接调查一下定价问题，可以拨款给注册预检项目的人，或者用其他方式削减开支。
note: 大多数乘客都在毫无必要的排队中煎熬，预检通道没有充分发挥作用，运管局不能再继续把资源挪用至此。

en:
1702
“The ancient Hawaiians were astronomers,” wrote Queen Liliuokalani, Hawaii’s last reigning monarch, in 1897. (14 words)
cn: 早就该好好运作这个预检计划了。
note: 1897年，夏威夷最后一位执政君主利留卡拉妮女王写道：“古夏威夷人都是天文学家”。

en: Sadly, all is not well with astronomy in Hawaii today. (10 words)
cn: 观星者曾是夏威夷社会上最受尊敬的人。
note: 遗憾的是，现在，在夏威夷，天文学的处境却不尽如人意。

en: At issue is the TMT’s planned location on Mauna Kea, a dormant volcano worshiped by some Hawaiians as the piko, that connects the Hawaiian Islands to the heavens. (28 words)
cn: 三十米级望远镜（TMT）是一座巨型的天文台，很可能会彻底改变人类的宇宙观。但因反对建设该望远镜，爆发了抗议活动。
note: 争议的问题在于，三十米级望远镜的规划选址位于莫纳克亚山：一座休眠火山，一些夏威夷人奉为神山，认为它是连接夏威夷群岛与天空的脐带。

en: Rested in the Pacific Ocean, Mauna Kea’s peak rises above the bulk of our planet’s dense atmosphere, where conditions allow telescopes to obtain images of unsurpassed clarity. (27 words)
cn: 但莫纳克亚山山上也安装了多台世界上最强大望远镜。
note: 莫纳克亚山坐落在太平洋上，其顶峰拔地而起，高耸入云，穿过浓密的大气层；这里的条件能让望远镜拍到无与伦比的清晰图像。

en: A small but vocal group of Hawaiians and environmentalists have long viewed their presence as disrespect for sacred land and a painful reminder of the occupation of what was once a sovereign nation. (33 words)
cn: 反对在莫纳克亚山上建造望远镜并不是什么新鲜事情。
note: 有少数敢于直言的夏威夷人和环保主义者长期以来一直都认为这些望远镜的存在亵渎了圣土，触到了他们的痛处，提醒他们想起曾经的主权国家已被占领。

en: In their eagerness to build bigger telescopes, they forgot that science is not the only way of understanding the world. (20 words)
cn: 近期发生争执，部分责任归咎于天文学家。
note: 他们急于建造望远镜，但忘记了科学并非是理解世界的唯一途径。

en: Hawaiian culture is not a relic of the past; it is a living culture undergoing a renaissance today. (18 words)
cn: 莫纳克亚山的生态系统极其脆弱，但其对于岛上居民具有神圣地位，他们没有始终优先考虑到这些问题。
note: 夏威夷文化不是古风遗俗；而是一种鲜活的文化，当前正在复兴。

en: The same curiosity to find what lies beyond the horizon that first brought early Polynesians to Hawaii’s shores inspires astronomers today to explore the heavens. (25 words)
cn: 然而科学也有其文化史，其根源可以追溯至文明肇始之初。
note: 最初，探索地平线之外的好奇心曾把早期的波利尼西亚人带到夏威夷海岸，如今，同样的好奇心驱使现在的天文学家去探索天空。

en: Perhaps that is why we explore the starry skies, as if answering a primal calling to know ourselves and our true ancestral homes. (23 words)
cn: 呼吁拆除莫纳克亚山上所有望远镜或者禁止未来在山上再建，都是罔顾事实，即：天文学和夏威夷文化这两者都在试图回答我们是谁、从何而来、要去何处等几个重大的问题。
note: 或许这就是我们探索星空的原因，好像在回应一个原始的呼唤，去了解我们自己，了解我们祖先的真正家园。

en: The TMT site was chosen to minimize the telescope’s visibility around the island and to avoid archaeological and environmental impact. (20 words)
cn: 天文学界也在让步，改变其对莫纳克亚山的利用方式。
note: 三十米级望远镜的选址尽量缩小，避免在全岛都能看得见望远镜，同时避免对考古和环境造成影响。

en: There is no reason why everyone cannot be welcomed on Mauna Kea to embrace their cultural heritage and to study the stars. (22 words)
cn: 为限制莫纳克亚山上的望远镜数量，旧的在达到使用年限后将被移除，其原址会恢复到自然状态。
note: 完全有理由欢迎所有人到莫纳克亚，拥抱他们的文化遗产，探索星空。

en: With Britain voting to leave the European Union, and GDP already predicted to slow as a result, it is now a timely moment to assess what he was referring to. (30 words)
cn: 罗伯特·F·肯尼迪曾说，一个国家的GDP（国内生产总值）可以衡量“一切，但却衡量不出人生价值的所在”。
note: 英国公投脱欧，有人已经预测GDP会因此下滑，现在正是考量肯尼迪这句话意欲所指的最佳时机。

en: Many argue that it is a flawed concept. (8 words)
cn: 国内生产总值（GDP）及其实用性问题，半个多世纪来一直困扰着政策制定者。
note: 很多人认为，这个概念存在缺陷。

en: By most recent measures, the UK’s GDP has been the envy of the Western world, with record low unemployment and high growth figures. (23 words)
cn: 它衡量的是无关痛痒的事情，却忽略了至关重要的事情。
note: 根据最近的评估，英国的国内生产总值一直都是西方国家羡慕的对象，增长数据有史以来最高，同时失业率最低。

en: A recent annual study of countries and their ability to convert growth into well-being sheds some light on that question. (20 words)
cn: 如果诸事都如此顺遂，那为什么尽管有很多人警告说脱欧会影响英国经济前景，还是有 1700多万人投票支持脱欧？
note: 一份最近的年度报告或许能拨开这个问题的迷雾，该报告分析了不同国家及其将数据增长转化为社会福利的能力。

en: Rather than just focusing on GDP, over 40 different sets of criteria from health, education and civil society engagement have been measured to get a more rounded assessment of how countries are performing. (33 words)
cn: 在所有评估的163个国家中，英国在确保经济增长能促进民生重大改善方面，表现最差。
note: 该报告并没有只关注国内生产总值，而是从健康、教育、公民社会参与度等40 多套不同的标准来进行衡量，从而更加全面评估各国的表现。

en: Yes, there has been a budding economic recovery since the 2008 global crash, but in key indicators in areas such as health and education, major economies have continued to decline. (30 words)
cn: 尽管所有的这些国家都面临各自的挑战，但仍有很多主题得一致面对。
note: 的确，自从2008年全球经济危机以来，已经有经济复苏迹象萌芽，但是在健康和教育等领域的关键指标上，几大经济体还在继续下滑。

en: Some relatively poor European countries have seen huge improvements across measures including civil society, income equality and the environment. (19 words)
cn: 然而，并非所有国家的情况都如此。
note: 欧洲一些相对贫穷的国家在公民社会、收入公平和环境等诸多方面都有巨大的改善。

en: So, what Kennedy was referring to was that while GDP has been the most common method for measuring the economic activity of nations, as a measure, it is no longer enough. (31 words)
cn: 这也是一些富裕国家需要学习的经验：当国内生产总值不再是衡量国家成功与否的唯一标准，这个世界会看起来就会大不相同。
note: 因为，肯尼迪的意思是，尽管国内生产总值一直是衡量国家经济活动的最常用的方法，但作为一种衡量标准，它还远远不够。

en: The sharp hit to growth predicted around the world and in the UK could lead to a decline in the everyday services we depend on for our well-being and for growth. (31 words)
cn: 它并不包括环境质量和教育成果这样的重要因素——所有的这些因素才会给人们带来幸福感。
note: 英国及世界各国都预测经济增长会受到重创，这会导致维持我们幸福生活和经济增长所倚靠的日常服务水平下降。

en:
1704
In a rare unanimous ruling, the US Supreme Court has overturned the corruption conviction of a former Virginia governor, Robert McDonnell. (21 words)
cn: 但是如果政策制定者集中精力提高社会福利，而不只是担心国内生产总值的数据，有可能避免注定的悲剧，或许甚至还能看到进步。
note: 美国最高法院罕见地一致裁决，推翻了对弗吉尼亚州前州长罗伯特·麦克唐纳的贪腐定罪。

en: The high court’s decision said the judge in Mr. McDonnell’s trial failed to tell a jury that it must look only at his “official acts,” or the former governor’s decisions on “specific” and “unsettled” issues related to his duties. (39 words)
cn: 但做出裁决的同时，对他的道德行为也嗤之以鼻：一家公司想找机会接近政府官员，他从那家公司那里捞了一块劳力士手表和一辆法拉利跑车等礼物。
note: 高院的裁决认为，麦克唐纳德先生一案中，法官未能告诉陪审团只能审查其“公务”行为：即在涉及与其公务职责有关的“具体”、“待定”的问题上，这个前任州长是如何决策的。

en: The court did suggest that accepting favors in return for opening doors is “distasteful” and “nasty.” (16 words)
cn: 法官裁决，只是帮助送礼的人接近其他政府官员，如果没有明确意图要给这些官员施压，不算受贿。
note: 法院表示，开后门，收礼物，的确是“卑鄙下流，令人恶心”。

en: Simply arranging a meeting, making a phone call, or hosting an event is not an “official act”. (17 words)
cn: 但是根据反贿赂法，证据必须是具体的利益，比如帮人批了一份合同或者法规。
note: 只是安排见了个面，打了个电话，搞了个活动，不算“公务行为”。

en: Elected leaders must be allowed to help supporters deal with bureaucratic problems without fear of prosecution for bribery. (18 words)
cn: 法院对此案的裁决，界定它是无罪偏袒行为，在法律上是合理的。
note: 应该允许当选的领导去帮助其支持者解决官僚问题，而不必担心被控受贿。

en: But the ruling reinforces the need for citizens and their elected representatives, not the courts, to ensure equality of access to government. (22 words)
cn: 首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨在法庭报告中提到，“奠定代议制政府基础的基本契约认定，公职人员应该倾听选民心声，为他们排忧解难。”
note: 但是法院的裁决强调，有必要由市民和他们选出的代表，而不是法庭，来确保公平接触政府部门。

en: This type of integrity requires well-enforced laws in government transparency, such as records of official meetings, rules on lobbying, and information about each elected leader’s source of wealth. (28 words)
cn: 决不能允许政府官员仅因为个人或者团体提供了竞选资助或者个人礼物，就在提供信息或者安排会面方面厚此薄彼。
note:
这种廉正建设需要充分贯彻法律，实现政务透明，比如官方会谈记录、游说
政策、每个当选领导人的财产来源信息。

en: But it is not always corruption. (6 words)
cn: 公务会面中的偏袒行为会煽动公众的情绪，认为是腐败。
note: 但并不总是腐败。

en: If connections can be bought, a basic premise of democratic society—that all are equal in treatment by government—is undermined. (21 words)
cn: 相反，公务员必须避免采用双重标准：用不同的方式接待老百姓和有钱人。
note: 如果这种联系可以收买，民主社会的基本前提——在政府代遇上人人平等——就被破坏了。

en: The court’s ruling is a step forward in the struggle against both corruption and official favoritism. (16 words)
cn: 善政良治有赖于充分理解每一个人内在价值。
note: 在与贪污腐败、公务偏袒的斗争中，最高法院的裁决是一次进步。

en: Don’t dismiss that possibility entirely. (5 words)
cn: 中产阶级面临的众多烦人的事情中，有一个可能在下一届总统竞选中并不会提及：如果机器人来抢工作了，会发生什么？
note: 不要以为完全不可能。

en: Lower-income jobs like gardening or day care don’t appeal to robots. (11 words)
cn: 哈佛大学的一项研究表明，美国大约有一半的工作都很可能实现自动化，中产阶级面临极大的风险，受到排挤。
note: 低收入的工作，像园艺或者日托等，机器人不感兴趣。

en: The rich own the robots, so they will be fine. (10 words)
cn: 但是很多中产阶级做的工作，比如火车驾驶、理财咨询、软件工程等，已经引起他们的关注了，或者很快就会。
note: 机器人是富人的，他们没事。

en: Optimists point out that technological upheaval has benefited workers in the past. (12 words)
cn: 这不是危言耸听。
note: 乐观主义者认为，技术变革在过去就曾让劳动者受益。

en: Likewise, automation should eventually boost productivity, stimulate demand by driving down prices, and free workers from hard, boring work. (19 words)
cn: 工业革命因为卢德之流发展并不太顺利，他们的工作完全被机械化织布机取代；但工业革命最终提高了生活水平；相比起曾毁掉的工作，创造的工作更多。
note: 同样，自动化最终也会推动生产力，通过拉低价格以刺激需求，并将工人从繁重、枯燥的工作中解放出来。

en: The first step, as Erik Brynjolfsson and Andrew McAfee argue in The Second Machine Age, should be rethinking education and job training. (22 words)
cn: 但是在自动化的发展中期，中产阶级可能需要很多帮助来做出调整。
note: 第一步，正如埃里克·布伦乔尔森和安德鲁·迈卡菲在其《第二次机械革命》一书中论述的那样，应该是反思教育和职业培训。

en: Vocational schools should do a better job of fostering problem-solving skills and helping students work alongside robots. (17 words)
cn: 学校的课程，从小学到大学，都应该发展成为少注重死记硬背，多注重创造力和复杂的沟通能力。
note: 职业学校应该更好的培养学生解决问题的能力，更好的帮助他们与机器人协同工作。

en: It could make extra training and instruction affordable. (8 words)
cn: 在线教育是传统教育的补充。
note: 它使大家都能负担得起额外的培训和辅导。

en: The challenge of coping with automation underlines the need for the U.S. to revive its fading business dynamism: Starting new companies must be made easier. (25 words)
cn: 想要掌握新技术的专业人员，无需负债也将得偿所愿。
note: 应对自动化带来的挑战凸显出美国有必要恢复其日益衰退的商业活力。

en: The best uses of 3D printers and virtual reality haven’t been invented yet. (13 words)
cn: 在以前的技术大变革时代，企业家就想到把劳动力和机器结合起来的方式，实现了平稳过度。
note: 三维打印机和虚拟现实迄今为止还没有得到充分应用。

en: Finally, because automation threatens to widen the gap between capital income and labor income, taxes and the safety net will have to be rethought. (24 words)
cn: 美国需要新公司来实现这些技术。
note: 最后，因为自动化可能会加深资本收入和劳动收入之间的鸿沟，所以必须得重新考虑税收和安全保障体系等问题。

en: Technology will improve society in ways big and small over the next few years, yet this will be little comfort to those who find their lives and careers upended by automation. (31 words)
cn: 缩减低收入劳工的税收，扩大劳动所得税抵扣等工资补贴：这将会提高收入、鼓励工作、奖励公司创造就业，并减少不平等现象。
note: 技术在未来几年多少都会促进社会发展，然而对于自动化已经颠覆了其生活和事业的人来说，这几乎不是什么安慰。

en: But policies to help workers adapt will be indispensable. (9 words)
cn: 捣毁抢走我们工作的机器是疯狂之举。
note: 但帮助工人适应的政策则是不可或缺的。

en: The implication is that Millennials prefer news from the White House to be filtered through other sources, not a president’s social media platform. (23 words)
cn: 哈佛大学一项最新的调查发现，超过三分之二的美国年轻人不赞成特朗普总统使用推特。
note: 这表明，千禧一代更希望白宫消息由其他途径发布，而不是总统的社交平台。

en: Yet as distrust has risen toward all media, people may be starting to beef up their media literacy skills. (19 words)
cn: 大多数美国人都靠社交媒体来查看每天的头条新闻。
note: 然而对所有媒体都越来越不信任，人们可能正在开始提高自己对媒体鉴别能力。

en: During the 2016 presidential campaign, nearly a quarter of web content shared by Twitter users in the politically critical state of Michigan was fake news, according to the University of Oxford. (31 words)
cn: 这样的情形，势在必行。
note: 根据牛津大学的研究，2016年的总统竞选期间，在具有重要政治意义的密歇根州，推特用户分享的网页内容有大约四分之一是虚假新闻。

en: Young people who are digital natives are indeed becoming more skillful at separating fact from fiction in cyberspace. (18 words)
cn: 并且，百资得新闻公司进行的一项调查发现，有 44%的脸书用户对来自该媒体巨头的新闻几乎不信任，或者根本不信任。
note: 年轻人是数字土著，他们的确是越来越善于在网络空间分辨真假。

en: They cross-check sources and prefer news from different perspectives—especially those that are open about any bias. (17 words)
cn:
奈特基金会对14到24岁的年轻人做了一次专题调查，发现他们用“分布式
信任”来验证消息真假。
note:
他们多方核对信息来源，更喜欢有观点的新闻，尤其是那种对任何偏见都能
兼容并包的新闻。

en: Such active research can have another effect. (7 words)
cn: 调查得出结论，“很多年轻人把进行自我教育，积极寻求对立观点作为自己的主要责任”。
note: 这种积极的探寻可能产生另一种效果。

en: Social media allows users to experience news events more intimately and immediately while also permitting them to re-share news as a projection of their values and interests. (27 words)
cn: 威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校在2014年对澳大利亚、英国和美国进行了一次调查，发现年轻人对社交媒体的依赖会让他们更多的了解政治。
note: 社交媒体使用户更加及时深入地体验新闻事件，同时他们还能够转发分享，表达自己的价值观和兴趣。

en: A survey by Barna research group found the top reason given by Americans for the fake news phenomenon is “reader error,” more so than made-up stories or factual mistakes in reporting. (31 words)
cn: 这会促使用户在其传递信息的时候要更注意自己的作用。
note: 巴纳研究小组的一项调查发现，美国人认为出现虚假新闻这种现象的首要原因是“读者失误”，而不是报道中的乱编故事或者内容失实。

en: In other words, the choice to share news on social media may be the heart of the issue. (18 words)
cn: 大约有三分之一的人认为，虚假新闻的问题在于通过社交媒体“误解或者夸大了新闻真相”。
note: 换言之，选择在社交媒体上转发分享新闻或许是问题的核心。

en: So when young people are critical of an over-tweeting president, they reveal a mental discipline in thinking skills—and in their choices on when to share on social media. (29 words)
cn: 巴纳集团总编罗克珊·斯通说：“这表明，在解决这一问题过程中确实存在一个个人的责任问题”。
note: 所以，年轻人在批判总统发推太多的时候，展现出他们在思维上，以及选择何时在社交媒体上分享的自制力。

en: DeepMind is one of the leading artificial intelligence (AI) companies in the world. (13 words)
cn: 对于英国国家医疗服务局（NHS）和深度思维公司（DeepMind）之间协议的种种危害，任何公正的评价都得首先承认双方本义是好的。
note: 深度思维是世界上顶尖的人工智能公司。

en: It is against that background that the information commissioner, Elizabeth Denham, has issued her damning verdict against the Royal Free hospital trust under the NHS, which handed over to DeepMind the records of 1.6 million patients in 2015 on the basis of a vague agreement which took far too little account of the patients’ rights and their expectations of privacy. (60 words)
cn: 这一成果用到医保上，潜力巨大；但也可能会导致权力进一步集中到科技巨头上面。
note: 事情发生的背景是，信息专员伊丽莎白·德纳姆对国家医疗服务局下属的皇家自由信托公司做出有罪裁决，因为该公司在2015年仅凭一份模糊的协议就把160万病人的档案交给了深度思维公司，这份协议严重忽视了患者的权利及其期望得到的隐私保护。

en: The NHS trust has mended its ways. (7 words)
cn: 深度思维基本上已经道歉了。
note: 国家医疗服务局也已改过自新。

en: There are lessons about informed patient consent to learn. (9 words)
cn: 国民医疗服务局和深度思维今后的协议，或许还有很多，都会受到严格的审查，以确保获得患者所有必要的许可，并删除所有不必要的数据。
note: 患者的知情同意权，我们要吸取教训。

en: Ms Denham chose to concentrate the blame on the NHS trust, since under existing law it “controlled” the data and DeepMind merely “processed” it. (24 words)
cn: 但是在本案中，隐私不是唯一的着眼点，甚至都不是最重要的。
note: 德纳姆女士集中火力攻击国家医疗局的信托公司，因为根据现行法律，它“掌控”数据，深度思维只能“处理”数据。

en: The great question is who should benefit from the analysis of all the data that our lives now generate. (19 words)
cn: 但是这种区分忽略了一点：正是处理并聚合信息赋予数据价值的，而非只是拥有信息。
note: 关键问题在于，分析我们现在生活中出现的所有数据，谁该从中获利？

en: That misses the way the surveillance economy works. (8 words)
cn: 隐私法设立的依据在于，个人识别信息会对其造成伤害。
note: 但它并没有理解监察经济的运作模式。

en: The use of privacy law to curb the tech giants in this instance feels slightly maladapted. (16 words)
cn: 在此，个人数据只有在与成千上万不计其数的其他人的信息相比较之下才能体现其价值。
note: 在这一案例中，用隐私法来约束科技巨头，感觉略有不妥。

en: It is not enough to say that the algorithms DeepMind develops will benefit patients and save lives. (17 words)
cn: 这样做，不能解决真正的担忧。
note: 深度思维公司开发的算法有利于患者，还能挽救生命，但是光讲这一点还不够。

en: If software promises to save lives on the scale that drugs now can, big data may be expected to behave as a big pharma has done. (26 words)
cn: 重要的是，这些算法属于私人垄断企业，但却用了公共资源开发出来的。
note: 如果软件有望能像药物现在那样治病救人，那么大数据或许有望能像制药公司那样发挥作用。

en: A long struggle will be needed to avoid a future of digital feudalism. (13 words)
cn: 我们还在这场革命的起始阶段，现在小小的选择或许在今后产生大大的影响。
note: 为避免未来出现数字封建主义，需要付出长期的努力。

en:
1804
The U.S. Postal Service (USPS) continues to bleed red ink. (10 words)
cn: 德纳姆女士的报告是一个良好的开端。
note: 美国邮政署（USPS）还在亏钱。

en: Meanwhile, it has more than $120 billion in unfunded liabilities, mostly for employee health and retirement costs. (17 words)
cn: 2016财年，其公布的净亏损额高达56亿美元，已经连续10年入不敷出了。
note: 同时，它还有超过1200亿美元的无资金准备债务，主要是员工的医疗费和退休金。

en: 从根本上说，美国邮政署处于一种历史的夹缝之中：一边是技术革新使其核心业务，普通邮件的需求量下滑，永无回升之力；一边是监管架构使其管理无法变通，不能针对新的现实来调整其运营方式。
cn: Fundamentally, the USPS is in a historic squeeze between technological change that has permanently decreased demand for its bread-and-butter product, first-class mail, and a regulatory structure that denies management the flexibility to adjust its operations to the new reality. (39 words)
note: And interest groups ranging from postal unions to greeting-card makers exert self-interested pressure on the USPS’s ultimate overseer—Congress—insisting that whatever else happens to the Postal Service, aspects of the status quo they depend on get protected. (38 words)

en: 这也解释了为什么最近几年一再努力，改革立法，均以失败告终，只留下邮政署无力支付账单，不得不推迟其至关重要的现代化改革。
cn: This is why repeated attempts at reform legislation have failed in recent years, leaving the Postal Service unable to pay its bills except by deferring vital modernization. (27 words)
note: Now comes word that everyone involved—Democrats, Republicans, the Postal Service, the unions and the system’s heaviest users—has finally agreed on a plan to fix the system. (28 words)

en: 众议院正要进行立法，这项法案预估能使美国邮政署在五年内节省大约286亿美元，这些钱能帮助他们购买新的运输工具，还可以花在其他维持生存的措施上。
cn: Legislation is moving through the House that would save USPS an estimated $28.6 billion over five years, which could help pay for new vehicles, among other survival measures. (28 words)
note: Most of the money would come from a penny-per-letter permanent rate increase and from shifting postal retirees into Medicare. (19 words)

en: 后一项举措将会大大抵消每年为退休人员预付医保而带来的财务负担，这样也解决了美国邮政署及其公会长期抱怨的问题。
cn: The latter step would largely offset the financial burden of annually pre-funding retiree health care, thus addressing a long-standing complaint by the USPS and its unions. (26 words)
note: If it clears the House, this measure would still have to get through the Senate—where someone is bound to point out that it amounts to the bare, bare minimum necessary to keep the Postal Service afloat, not comprehensive reform. (40 words)

en: 美国邮政署内的集体谈判没有任何变化，考虑到员工支出占到公司成本的80%，这是一个重大遗漏。
cn: There’s no change to collective bargaining at the USPS, a major omission considering that personnel accounts for 80 percent of the agency’s costs. (23 words)
note: Also missing is any discussion of eliminating Saturday letter delivery. (10 words)

cn: As degrees became universal, they became devalued (just as governments hiked up the cost of getting one). (17 words)
note: Today, 28 per cent of graduates in the UK are in non-graduate roles; a percentage which is double the average amongst the OECD. (23 words)

en: Employers have long seen the advantages of hiring school leavers who often prove themselves to be more committed and loyal employees than graduates. (23 words)
cn: Thankfully, there are signs that this is already happening, with Gen Z seeking to learn from their millennial predecessors, even if parents and teachers tend to be still set in the degree mindset. (33 words)
note: Many too are seeing the advantages of scrapping a degree requirement for certain roles—just as Penguin did in 2016. (20 words)

en: Postgraduates now earn 40 per cent more than graduates. (9 words)
cn: In this age of generalists, it pays to have specific knowledge or skills. (13 words)
note: When more and more of us have a degree, it makes sense (albeit expensive) to have two. (17 words)

en: Education, and not just knowledge gained on campus, will be a core part of Generation Z’s career trajectory. (18 words)
cn: It has been estimated that this generation due to the pressures of technology, the wish for personal fulfilment and desire for diversity will work for 17 different employers over the course of their working life and have five different careers. (40 words)
note: I have often heard older generations talk about their degree (even if it was gained decades ago) in the present and personal tense: “I am a geographer or ‘I am a classist’. (32 words)

en: These were some of the words that Nature readers used to describe their experiences of art-science collaborations in a series of articles on partnerships between artists and researchers. (28 words)
cn:
2203
Enlightening, stimulating, inspiring, fun. (4 words)
note: Nearly 40% of the roughly 350 people who responded to an accompanying poll said they had collaborated with artists; and almost all said they would consider doing so in future. (30 words)

en: Scientists are increasingly seeking out visual artists and designers to help them to communicate their work to new audiences. (19 words)
cn: Public engagement has become essential to many research projects. (9 words)
note: “Artists help scientists reach a broader audience and make emotional connections that enhance learning,” one respondent said. (17 words)

en: One example of how artists and scientists have together rocked the senses came last month when the Sydney Symphony Orchestra in Australia performed a reworked version of Antonio Vivaldi’s The Four Seasons. (32 words)
cn: “There’s often a visual aspect to my science that generating and publishing data does not convey.” (16 words)
note: They reimagined the 300-year-old score by injecting the latest climate prediction data for each season — provided by Monash University’s Climate Change Communication Research Hub in Melbourne. (26 words)

en: But a genuine partnership must be a two-way street. (9 words)
cn: The performance was a creative call to action ahead of November’s United Nations. (13 words)
note: Fewer artists than scientists responded to the Nature poll; however, several respondents noted that artists do not simply assist scientists with their communication requirements. (24 words)

en: Such an approach can both prompt new research as well as result in powerful art. (15 words)
cn: The alliances are most valuable when scientists and artists have a shared stake in a project, are able to jointly design it and can critique each other’s work. (28 words)
note: More than half a century ago, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) opened its Center for Advanced Visual Studies (CAVS) in Cambridge to explore the role of technology in culture. (30 words)

en: They deliberately focused their projects around light — hence the ‘visual studies’ in the name. (14 words)
cn: Its founders believed that artists and scientists could, together, create a vision for a more humane world. (17 words)
note: Light was a something that both artists and scientists had an interest in, and therefore could form the basis of collaboration, says Seth Riskin, a visual-arts researcher at the MIT Museum who previously worked at CAVS. (36 words)

en:
2204
The personal grievance provisions of the Employment Relations Act 2000 (ERA) prevent  
an employer from firing an employee without good cause. (22 words)
cn: Artists and scientists alike are immersed in discovery and invention, and challenge and critique are core to both, too. (19 words)
note: Instead, dismissals must be justified. (5 words)

en: The premise was that the common law of contract lacked sufficient safeguards for workers against arbitrary conduct by management. (19 words)
cn: Personal grievance procedures were designed to guard the jobs of ordinary workers from “unjustified dismissals”. (15 words)
note: Long gone are the days when a boss could simply give an employee contractual notice. (15 words)

en: The difference between C-grade and A-grade managers may very well be the difference between business success or failure. (18 words)
cn: As countless boards and business owners will attest, constraining firms from firing poorly performing, high-earning managers is a handbrake on boosting productivity and overall performance. (25 words)
note: Between preserving the jobs of ordinary workers or losing them. (10 words)

en: If not placing jobs at risk, to the extent employment protection laws constrain business owners from dismissing underperforming managers, those laws act as a constraint on firm productivity and therefore on workers’ wages. (33 words)
cn: Consequently — and paradoxically — laws introduced to protect the jobs of ordinary workers may be placing those jobs at risk. (19 words)
note: Indeed, in “An International Perspective on New Zealand’s Productivity Paradox” (2014), the Productivity Commission singled out the low quality of managerial capabilities as a cause of the country’s poor productivity growth record. (32 words)

en: This makes it harder for the marginal manager to gain employment. (11 words)
cn: Because employment protection laws make it costlier to fire an employee, employers are more cautious about hiring new staff. (19 words)
note: And firms pay staff less because firms carry the burden of the employment arrangement going wrong. (16 words)

en: Across the Tasman, Australia deals with the unjustified dismissal paradox by excluding employees earning above a specified “high-income threshold” from the protection of its unfair dismissal laws. (27 words)
cn: Stringent job dismissal regulations adversely affect productivity growth and hamper both prosperity and overall wellbeing. (15 words)
note: In New Zealand, a 2016 private members’ Bill tried to permit firms and high-income employees to contract out of the unjustified dismissal regime. (23 words)

en: Pat Hardy, who sympathises with the views of the energy sector, is resisting proposed changes to science standards for pre-teen pupils. (21 words)
cn:
2301
The weather in Texas may have cooled since the recent extreme heat, but the temperature will be high at the State Board of Education meeting in Austin this month as officials debate how climate change is taught in Texas schools. (40 words)
note: “There are as many scientists working against all the panic of global climate change as there are those who are pushing it,” she claims. (24 words)

en: Most scientists and experts sharply dispute Hardy’s views. (8 words)
cn: You need to remember where your bread is buttered.”(9 words)
note: Board members like her “casually dismiss the career work of scholars and scientists as just another misguided opinion,” says Dan Quinn, senior communications strategist at the Texas Freedom Network, a non-profit group that monitors public education. (36 words)

en: Among the 10 worst performers were some of the most populous states, including Texas, which was given the lowest grade (F) and has a disproportionate influence because its textbooks are widely sold elsewhere. (33 words)
cn: A study last year by the National Center for Science Education, a non-profit group of scientists and teachers, looking at how state public schools across the country address climate change in science classes, gave barely half of US states a grade B+ or higher. (44 words)
note: Glenn Branch, the centre’s deputy director, cautions that setting state-level science standards is only one limited benchmark in a country that decentralises decisions to local school boards. (27 words)

en: It is also less prominent in many social studies courses. (10 words)
cn: Another issue is that, while climate change is well integrated into some subjects and at some ages—such as earth and space sciences in high schools—it is not as well represented in curricula for younger children and in subjects that are more widely taught, such as biology and chemistry. (50 words)
note: Branch points out that, even if a growing number of official guidelines and textbooks reflect scientific consensus on climate change, unofficial educational materials that convey more biased perspectives are being distributed to teachers. (33 words)

en: Now, with record-high home prices and historically low inventory, there’s an increased urgency in such regulation, particularly among those who worry that developers will come in and buy up swaths of housing to flip for a fortune on the short-term rental market. (42 words)
cn:
2302
Communities throughout New England have been attempting to regulate short-term rentals since sites like Airbnb took off in the 2010s. (20 words)
note: In New Hampshire, where the rental vacancy rate has dropped below 1 percent, housing advocates fear unchecked short-term rentals will put further pressure on an already strained market. (28 words)

en: Without enough affordable housing in southern New Hampshire towns, “employers are having a hard time attracting employees, and workers are having a hard time finding a place to live,” Taylor said. (31 words)
cn: “We are at a crisis level on the supply of rental housing,” said Nick Taylor, executive director of the Workforce Housing Coalition of the Greater Seacoast. (26 words)
note: However, short-term rentals also provide housing for tourists, pointed out Ryan Castle, CEO of a local association of realtors. (19 words)

en: “I think individuals being able to rent out their second home is a good thing. (15 words)
cn: Short-term rentals themselves are not the crux of the issue, said Keren Horn, an expert on affordable housing policy. (19 words)
note: If it’s their vacation home anyway, and it’s just empty, why can’t you make money off it?” Horn said. (19 words)

en: At the end of 2018, Governor Charlie Baker of Massachusetts signed a bill to rein in those potential investor-buyers. (19 words)
cn: “I think the question is, shouldn’t a developer who’s really building a hotel, but disguising it as not a hotel, be treated and taxed and regulated like a hotel?” Horn said. (31 words)
note: The bill requires every rental host to register with the state, mandates they carry insurance, and opens the potential for local taxes on top of a new state levy. (29 words)

en: Both of these books are published next week by Penguin Random House (PRH), a company currently involved in a stand-off with Waterstones. (22 words)
cn:
Horn said similar registration requirements could benefit struggling cities and towns, but “if we want to make a change in the housing market, the main one is we have to build a lot more.” (34 words)

2303
If you’re heading for your nearest branch of Waterstones, the biggest book retailer in the UK, in search of the Duchess of Sussex’s new children’s book The Bench, you might have to be prepared to hunt around a bit; the same may be true of The President’s Daughter, the new thriller by Bill Clinton and James Patterson. (57 words)
note: The problem began late last year, when PRH confirmed that it had introduced a credit limit with Waterstones “at a very significant level”. (23 words)

en: We are not boycotting PRH titles but we are doing our utmost to ensure that availability for customers remains good despite the lower overall levels of stock. (27 words)
cn: PRH declined to comment on the issue, but a spokesperson for Waterstones told me: “Waterstones are currently operating with reduced credit terms from PRH, the only publisher in the UK to place any limitations on our ability to trade. (39 words)
note: We are hopeful with our shops now open again that normality will return and that we will be allowed to buy appropriately. (22 words)

en: In the meantime, PRH authors have been the losers. (9 words)
cn: The sales for our May Books of the Month surpassed any month since 2018.” (14 words)
note: Big-name PRH authors may suffer a bit, but it’s those mid-list authors, who normally rely on Waterstones staff’s passion for promoting books by lesser-known writers, who will be praying for an end to the dispute. (35 words)

en: “The publishing industry talks about diversity in terms of authors and staff but it also needs a plurality of ways of delivering intellectual contact, choice and different voices. (28 words)
cn: “This is all part of a wider change towards concentration of power,” says literary agent Andrew Lownie. (17 words)
note: After all, many of the most interesting books in recent years have come from small publishers.” (16 words)

en: Each year researchers publish millions of papers in more than 30,000 journals. (12 words)
cn:
2304
Scientific papers are the recordkeepers of progress in research. (9 words)
note: The scientific community measures the quality of those papers in a number of ways, including the perceived quality of the journal (as reflected by the title’s impact factor) and the number of citations a specific paper accumulates. (37 words)

en: In 2016, researchers developed an algorithm to recognize suspicious citation patterns, including groups of authors that disproportionately cite one another and groups of journals that cite each other frequently to increase the impact factors of their publications. (37 words)
cn: Citation cartels, where journals, authors, and institutions conspire to inflate citation numbers, have existed for a long time. (18 words)
note: Recently, another expression of this predatory behavior has emerged: so-called support service consultancies that provide language and other editorial support to individual authors and to journals sometimes advise contributors to add a number of citations to their articles. (38 words)

en: These journals can act as milk cows where every single article in an issue may cite a specific paper or a series of papers. (24 words)
cn: The birth of predatory journals wasn’t far behind. (8 words)
note: In some instances, there is absolutely no relationship between the content of the article and the citations. (17 words)

en: How insidious is this type of citation manipulation? (8 words)
cn: Such practices can lead an article to accrue more than 150 citations in the same year that it was published. (20 words)
note: In one example, an individual—acting as author, editor, and consultant—was able to use at least 15 journals as citation providers to articles published by five scientists at three universities. (31 words)

en: Scopus itself has all the data necessary to detect this malpractice. (11 words)
cn: In fact, a listing in Scopus seems to be a criterion to be targeted in this type of citation manipulation. (20 words)
note: Red flags include a large number of citations to an article within the first year. (15 words)
